betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Binnie's Genetics mod will allow you to pick and chose traits for bees, trees and Botany flowers. But not for free! Doing this comes at a price.

[note]Note: We'll be focusing just on the bees in this section.[/note]

You will need a few streams of resources you can automate into passive generation. These are used across the process in different machines for different purposes, but Growth Medium is a super-resource you can substitute for some of the steps if you're able to produce that in abundance.

The research process can be easily automated. Gene modification and drone generation can be automated, but are slow and infrequent enough that you will probably just attend to them when you need them.

The general idea is to sequence a pile of leftover drones until you get all the traits you want from it. You'll then have those traits forever and can inject your own combinations of everything you've found into drone Larvae to make new drones that carry those traits. Larvae hatch only into drones. You don't get to directly hatch princesses and queens, so you have to worry about these new traits sticking across generations.

These genetic edits don't let you create new traits out of thin air. They just let you pick and choose what you have already researched, and that has to come out of bees you already have. You may use this to set up all your production bees through the rest of the game, but traits like 4x fertility also help with breeding.

You'll want to prepare a lab workspace for this. The actual machines won't take too much space. In fact, you'll probably wind up smacking a bunch of them against world accelerators, which will require direct contact due to using Tile Entity mode. What will end up taking up more space is any passive resource generation you prepare to make the process more hands-free.

You will need an alveary with a hatchery to produce Larvae of the base bee you want to modify.

These machines require RF power. It's recommended to provide power to an Ender IO capacitor and power these machines with RF instead of powering directly with GT EU. You can connect GT EU power up to these, but you run into strange consumption from amperage conversion glitches, or machines not getting power despite looking like they have proper cabling.

Ultimately, you'll need at least 7 of these casings and could end up making 14+ casings for all the machines you may opt to produce. You will also need HV-tier circuit production. There is §oone§o§r EV circuit involved in the complete process too.

[note]We give special credit to Three for a video explaining all these machines and their interplay:

§oAn Early Game Guide to Genetically Modify your Bees in Gregtech: New Horizons§r
[url]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EmhDlga2SCA[/url]

Note that he will rant about the quest book. That was--ahem--an older version of the quest book.
[/note]
